---
title: "plotting_tree_in_R"
author: "ijjL."
date: "2026-04-19"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(ggtree)      # for tree + annotations
library(treeio)      # for reading IQ-TREE files
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ape)         # for drop.tip / keep.tip
library(pheatmap)    # for standalone heatmap
library(ggnewscale)  # needed for gheatmap layering
```

```{r}
raw <- read_lines("combine_gffs.txt")
```

# Split into blocks
```{r}
block_starts <- which(str_detect(raw, "^#FILE"))
block_ends   <- c(block_starts[-1] - 1, length(raw))
blocks       <- map2(block_starts, block_ends, ~ raw[.x:.y])
```

# Convert each block into a data frame
```{r}
parse_block <- function(block) {
  block <- block[block != ""]
  header_line <- str_remove(block[1], "^#")
  header <- str_split(header_line, "\t")[[1]]
  df <- map_df(block[-1], ~ {
    vals <- str_split(.x, "\t")[[1]]
    tibble(!!!set_names(as.list(vals), header))
  })
  df
}

amr_long <- map(blocks, parse_block) %>%
  bind_rows(.id = "block_id") %>%
  clean_names()
```

```{r}
amr_clean <- amr_long %>%
  mutate(across(-c(file, block_id), ~ na_if(., "."))) %>%
  mutate(across(-c(file, block_id), ~ str_replace_all(., ";", ","))) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = -c(file, block_id, num_found),
    names_to = "raw_gene",
    values_to = "identity"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(identity)) %>%
  separate_rows(identity, sep = ",") %>%
  mutate(identity = as.numeric(identity)) %>%
  mutate(gene = raw_gene %>%
           str_remove_all("^x_|^_") %>%
           str_remove_all("staphylococcus_aureus_") %>%
           str_remove_all("pc1_beta_lactamase_") %>%
           str_remove_all("\\(.*?\\)") %>%
           str_remove_all("_\\d+$") %>%
           str_remove_all("_number_sign") %>%
           str_replace_all("_+", "_") %>%
           str_trim() %>%
           str_to_lower()) %>%
  mutate(gene = case_when(
    str_detect(gene, "blaz|bla_z|bla_bla|bla_i_of_z|bla_r1|bla_bla_r1") ~ "blaZ",
    str_detect(gene, "^tet$|tet_tet|tet_?k") ~ "tetK",
    str_detect(gene, "nor_?a|nor_c") ~ "norA",
    str_detect(gene, "mec_?a") ~ "mecA",
    str_detect(gene, "erm_?c") ~ "ermC",
    str_detect(gene, "dfr_?g") ~ "dfrG",
    str_detect(gene, "dfr_?c") ~ "dfrC",
    str_detect(gene, "dfr_s1") ~ "dfrS1",
    str_detect(gene, "aac_?6.*aph_?2|aph_?2.*aac|aac6_?ie_?aph2|aac_6_ie_aph_2_ia_bifunctional_protein") ~ "aac(6')-aph(2'')",
    str_detect(gene, "a_gly_ap_h_stph|a_gly_str|a_gly_aad_c|str|aph_2_ii") ~ "aac(6')-aph(2'')",
    str_detect(gene, "arl_r") ~ "arlR",
    str_detect(gene, "arl_s") ~ "arlS",
    str_detect(gene, "mep_a") ~ "mepA",
    str_detect(gene, "mep_r") ~ "mepR",
    str_detect(gene, "mgr_a") ~ "mgrA",
    str_detect(gene, "sdr_m") ~ "sdrM",
    str_detect(gene, "sep_a") ~ "sepA",
    str_detect(gene, "kdp_d") ~ "kdpD",
    str_detect(gene, "lmr_s") ~ "lmrS",
    str_detect(gene, "phe_dha1") ~ "pheDha1",
    str_detect(gene, "^tet") ~ "tetK",
    TRUE ~ gene
  )) %>%
  mutate(gene = str_replace_all(gene, "_", "") %>% str_to_title()) %>%
  mutate(gene = case_when(
    gene == "Aminoglycosidemodifying" ~ "Aminoglycoside",
    TRUE ~ gene
  ))
```

# Final presence table
```{r}
amr_presence <- amr_clean %>%
  mutate(barcode = str_extract(file, "barcode[0-9]+")) %>%
  mutate(present = if_else(identity > 0, 1, 0)) %>%
  group_by(barcode, gene) %>%
  summarise(
    max_identity = max(identity, na.rm = TRUE),
    present      = max(present, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

# ── NEW: Prepare the heatmap matrix aligned to tree tips ──────────────────────

```{r}
# ---------- 1. Read & subset the tree ----------
tree <- read.tree("iqtree_results/core_tree.treefile")

keep_barcodes <- c("barcode04_all_reads",
                   "barcode09_all_reads",
                   "barcode19_all_reads",
                   "barcode24_all_reads")

tree_subset <- keep.tip(tree, keep_barcodes)
print(tree_subset)
```

```{r}
library(ape)

# 1. Define your desired order
desired_order <- c("barcode24", "barcode19", "barcode09", "barcode04")

# 2. Reorder the tree object before plotting
tree_subset <- rotateConstr(tree_subset, desired_order)

# 3. Now run your existing plot code
p_tree <- ggtree(tree_subset, branch.length = "branch.length") + 
  geom_tiplab(size = 3, hjust = -0.05) + 
  theme_tree2() + 
  xlim(0, max(node.depth.edgelength(tree_subset)) * 1.6)

p_tree
```


```{r}
p_tree <- ggtree(tree_subset, branch.length = "branch.length") + 
  # 1. Show Bootstrap Values at the nodes
  # subset = !isTip ensures we only label internal branching points
  geom_text2(aes(label = label, subset = !isTip), 
             hjust = 1.2,   # Moves text slightly to the left of the node
             vjust = -0.5,  # Moves text slightly above the branch
             size = 3, 
             color = "darkred") +
  
  # Your previous layers
  geom_tiplab(aes(label = ""), align = TRUE, linetype = "dotted", color = "gray50") +
  geom_tippoint(size = 2, color = "black") +
  theme_tree2() + 
  xlim(0, max(node.depth.edgelength(tree_subset)) * 1.2)

p_tree
```


```{r}
# ---------- 2. Build a short-label tip map ----------
# Tree tips are like "barcode04_all_reads"; AMR data uses "barcode04"
# We create a mapping so tip labels match barcode IDs in amr_presence.

tip_map <- tibble(
  tip_label = tree_subset$tip.label,
  barcode   = str_extract(tip_label, "barcode[0-9]+")
)

# Rename tree tips to short barcode IDs so they match the heatmap rows
tree_subset$tip.label <- tip_map$barcode
```

```{r}
# ---------- 3. Pivot presence data into a wide matrix ----------
# Rows = barcodes (must match tree tip labels exactly)
# Columns = genes

mat <- amr_presence %>%
  filter(barcode %in% tree_subset$tip.label) %>%   # keep only barcodes in tree
  select(barcode, gene, present) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = gene, values_from = present, values_fill = 0) %>%
  column_to_rownames("barcode")

# Ensure row order matches the tree tip order
mat <- mat[tree_subset$tip.label, , drop = FALSE]

# Convert to numeric matrix (gheatmap requires this)
mat <- as.matrix(mat)
storage.mode(mat) <- "numeric"

head(mat)
```


```{r}
# 1. Extract the visual order of tips from the ggtree plot
# This ensures the heatmap matches exactly what you see on the screen
tip_order <- get_taxa_name(p_tree)

# 2. Reorder your matrix rows based on that order
mat_ordered <- mat[tip_order, , drop = FALSE]

# 3. Draw the heatmap with the new order
# Set cluster_rows = FALSE so it doesn't try to re-order them again
library(ComplexHeatmap)

Heatmap(mat_ordered, 
        cluster_rows = FALSE, 
        cluster_columns = FALSE,
        column_names_side = "top", 
        column_names_rot = 45,
        col = c("#f0f0f0", "#e31a1c"),
        name = "AMR Gene Presence",
        rect_gp = gpar(col = "white", lwd = 1),
        border = TRUE
)
```


```{r}
library(ComplexHeatmap)

Heatmap(mat_ordered, 
        cluster_rows = FALSE, 
        cluster_columns = FALSE,
        column_names_side = "top", 
        column_names_rot = 45,
        col = c("0" = "#f0f0f0", "1" = "#e31a1c"), # Explicitly mapping colors
        name = "AMR Gene Presence",
        rect_gp = gpar(col = "white", lwd = 1),
        border = TRUE,
        
        # --- Update Legend Labels Here ---
        heatmap_legend_param = list(
          at = c(0, 1), 
          labels = c("Absent", "Present")
        )
)
```




```{r}
library(ggtree)

# Basic tree without text
p_tree <- ggtree(tree_subset) 

# Add heatmap with automatically aligned grid lines
gheatmap(p_tree, mat_ordered, offset = 0.01, width = 3.5, 
         colnames_angle = 45, colnames_offset_y = 0.2)
```
